rationale: The citing paper centrally analyzes how replacing item price discounts with reward-point promotions changes store visits, spending, and revenue outcomes, while the cited paper provides a retailer-focused empirical framework on promotion effects and profitability (net unit vs. net profit impact). Given this clear topical/methodological proximity on promotion impact at the retailer level and the lack of in-text context to show it is merely incidental, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
